name: Davies, G. R.
motto: "You Cassius hath a lean and hungry look."
major: Valley City Normal, Des Moines College, Iowa, A. B. and A. M.; Graduate work at University of Wisconsin
description: A man of excellent parts, and wholly human. True and sincere as a friend, just as a pedagogue, his acquaintanceship is well worth the making. Mr. Davies is an excellent addition to the community.
text:
"You Cassius hath a lean and hungry look."

name: Egelhoff, Clarence Raymond
motto: "A dimpled smile that melts the hardest heart."
description: Both as student and instructor Mr. Egelhoff has made an enviable place for himself in the University. Anyone doubting his popularity with his students, would do well to examine the notebooks of some of the young ladies in his class.
text:
"A dimpled smile that melts the hardest heart."

name: Freeman, Benjamin Farrow
motto: "Others may disputations make; For me, I keep my counsel."
description: However, if you break the ice, he is always ready for a friendly discussion on any subject. His greatest delight is solving the mysteries of nature. The sight of a lignite briquet moves him, mentally and spiritually, more than anything else on this foot-stool.

name: Gustafson, Theodore Axel
motto: "Avaunt, thou devil, black and threatening, thou hast no dread for me."
major: University of Minnesota, U. N. D., B. A., '11
description: Here is proof of the statement that all the work is not done by those who make the big noise. The "power behind the throne" in the Y. M. C. A. this year. We admire his absolute impeccability of character—secretly.

name: Haug, Joseph L.
motto: "Silence is golden."
description: We know little about this man. He came in silence, he remained the same way, and now he has departed with still less ostentation. However, we have heard nothing against him, so he must be all right.
text:
"Silence is golden."

name: Koehn, George L.
motto: "Earth sounds my wisdom, and high heaven my fame."
description: His only unsolved problem is how the world will exist when he is gone. George came from Wisconsin for the purpose of civilizing the west and he fails to comprehend why his greatness is not appreciated. He "orates," debates, eulogizes La Follette, upholds the Republicanism of Taft, and falls a victim to the darts of Cupid.

name: Olson, Mabel Pauline
motto: "A woman good, without pretence, Blessed with plain reason and sober sense."
description: Mabel graduated from Arts in 1909. After teaching one year, she was irresistibly drawn back to take advanced work in Science, and is said to really revel in it. As that isn't enough to keep her busy, she assists in the Biology department.

name: Quirke, Terence T.
motto: "A merrier man Within the limit of becoming mirth, I never spent an hour's talk withal."
description: An Englishman who can, upon occasion, see the point of a joke. Two years ago he discovered that there is matter of interest in Geology and a year later that there may also be interesting types among the feminine gender, which goes to show that there must be something good about Geology.

name: Nesdahl, Elise
motto: "Science when well digested is nothing but good sense and reason."
description: Because she really thinks so, Elise is back with us this year taking nothing but science. And yet, strange to say, she is decidedly human. She is always ready to talk on any subject, but wouldn't for the world bore you with her knowledge.

name: Birdie, M. Adair
motto: "My attachments are strong attachments and never weaken."
description: If you know Birdie you would sooner have her on your side in an argument than with the opposition. In the era of Woman Suffrage Birdie will make an excellent ruler. She has already shown her ability by an uninterrupted term of four years as Dictator of a Mutual Admiration Society.

name: Lawrence, J. Anderson
motto: "He is as full of valour as of kindness."
description: Lawrence is a quiet fellow who never raises his voice but to bring harmony. He is our star baritone of Quartette and solo fame. His mind is as true as his voice; that his class records show. And his heart is as true as either; that will be sworn to by his friends.

name: Seymour, Eberhart Anderson
motto: "One vast substantial smile."
description: Seymour is one of the co-op's genial counter jumpers. A fusser of first magnitude—he knows every nook and cranny, every crack and crevice of the parlors of every girl's dorm on the campus, and incidentally every girl's first name. Always jolly, he is ever ready to share his joys and keep his sorrows to himself.

name: Bangs, Philip R.
high school: Grand Forks High School, Northwestern Military Academy
motto: "Love is like a dizziness, It winna let a poor man Gang about his business."
description: Phil is not a fusser; he has it down to too scientific a basis for that term. Whatever the term, Phil may shine as one and only perfect example. It was once rumored that he was destined to Phil-osophy. His study of humanity is limited to too few for that.

name: Baarson, Oscar A.
motto: "He smiled, and smiling hummed his tune."
description: Specializes in ceramics—but he is not made of common clay. A freshman's wit is measured by the time it takes him to "get on" to Baarson. The aurora borealis effulgence of Oscar's auburn locks is surpassed only by his popularity with the girls. Reported to be a good mixer—of drinks.

name: Bacon, William Cyril
motto: "He trod the ways of glory, And sounded all the depths and shoals of fame."
description: For four long years Bill has been "leading man" on all occasions. And the sad part of it is that he always looks the part—and many a lovelorn co-ed has looked furtively and pensively upon him, and even a member of the faculty has fallen before him. Bill is an authority on everything from "Nathan Hale's 'blood and blank verse'" to a "choregus by proxy."

name: Barnes, Helen
motto: "Miss Bangs, will you recite, please?" — Dr. Ladd.
description: Though a most quiet and demure appearing maiden, Helen's life at the University has been one long series of loud reports which might be summed up thusly: Bang! Bang! Bang! Bang! Bang! Bang! Bang! Bang!—and present indications point to The Bangs continuing on ad infinitum.

name: Beauchamp, Exzelia E.
motto: "O, come on, cheer up."
description: Exzelia does not make friends — she doesn't have to—everyone is just naturally her friend. Of her scholarship, it is enough to say that she has an affinity for A's, which tells volumes in this A-less era. It is rumored that she has been offered a position on the Herald staff.

name: Bennett, Charles Bigelow
motto: "Every why hath a wherefore."
description: Science is a source of much interest to this sharkey engineer. He fairly loves to take machines to pieces in order to discover their "why". If his curly locks were only gold instead of black he would scarcely be able to apply his favored head to study. To hear him talk about electricity would inflame one, either to be an engineer, or to brain the speaker in exasperation.

name: Asmunder, Benson
motto: "Wrapt in a measureless content."
clubs: Vice-President at large of Y. P. B.
description: A consistent worker—in spite of the fact that he is now taking law. A man of mighty vocabulary which he has some difficulty in concentrating on a given subject,—though he is perfectly willing to discuss anything from any point of view.

name: Fred, N. Bjerk en
high school: Kindred High School, Model High School
motto: "He is unconsciously correct."
description: A most earnest student. All indications show that he will carry into his chosen profession his deftness with carving tools attained in his former pursuit of carpentry. If attacked with appendicitis he would insist upon the use of local anaesthesia, so that he could watch his vivisection and have a glimpse of his inner self.

name: Ernest, James Budge
high school: Grand Forks High School, Valley City High School
motto: "For it's always fair weather when good fellows get together."
description: Ernie is the embodiment of leisure and good fellowship. Ernie without a smile would be about as probable as a sail without a ship. Ernie in a hurry would be just as probable as Ernie without a smile. A football man of magnitude,—both on and off the field.

name: Budge, Jessie M.
motto: "Devoted, generous, void of guile; With her whole heart's welcome in her smile."
description: Jessie is a "hard one" to write up because she says so little and does so much. Beneath that guileless exterior there is unlimited executive ability, as well as unlimited possibilities for fun and friendship. Her freshman scholarship record has carried her along through three years, while she has taken care of University affairs in general.

name: Carr, Andy M.
high school: Minot High School, Occidental College, Los Angeles, Cal.
motto: "And is a Welsh man, Andy's sure of Grace. Andy is a doctor, And always has a case."
description: One of those clever little fellows to whom the wooing of hearts comes as natural as living. Married—well, no but—almost. His lesser troubles are music and medicine—and Dr. Young. He is level-headed and has a broad perspective—his size belies him surely.

name: Cosgrove, Dennis Edward
description: Strictly conservative, but a versatile man withal—witness his scholastic shiftiness. Starting as a wielder of the slide-rule (or slip-stick, if you like that term better) he has risen—or is it sunk?—through the successive degrees of Engineering and Arts, finally discovering his real vocation in Law.

name: Crary, J. Burton
motto: "Poets are all who love and feel great truths and tell them. That's me."
description: Much can be said about Burt among the little ones of the girls. He is a clean-cut fellow, has a journalistic trend, and composes songs on the side. Still, Burt takes life seriously and laughs at his own jokes—alone. Not a shark, but a practitioner at bluff.

name: Crombie, W. Ransom
motto: "Every laddie has his lassie."
description: His escapades in electrical measurements vary from the practical experience of attempting to carry the University's lighting current through his body to the delicately theoretical task of trying to conceal from Doc Taylor what he doesn't know. His advice to engineers is: "Never ask 'Doc' a question unless you know the answer."

name: Cunningham, Gertrude
motto: "Pretty to walk with, Witty to talk with, And pleasant to think on."
description: Gert has bobbed her head through four fruitful college years. A dainty maiden with the power of doing all things well. Has been the heroine of many a brave knight's romance both on and off the stage. At present is leading lady before a small but admiring audience of one.

name: Dahl, Thorvald
motto: "'Tis deeds must win the prize."
description: Thor is not the god of war, in spite of his name. Even so, he would be a bad man to start a fight with. He is an athlete, but that does not keep him from being a student and a speaker. In fact he is a well-rounded and versatile man. (By rounded, we don't mean to suggest that he is a rounder, far be it from such.)

name: Dickson, Allie
motto: "What she undertook she did, and that is more than a great many of us do; not to say anything of her doing it as well as it could be done."
description: Allie does everything from leading the Junior Prom and playing basketball to monopolizing the A's and winning a scholarship. Is it any wonder that all one sees of her is a streak as she hurries across the campus?

name: Dougherty, Irene M.
motto: "A face lighted up by the loveliest pair of blue eyes!"
description: There is a saying which speaks of being merry and wise. Some people can be merry and can't be wise, but here is one who is both. Just listen to Irene in class! But if you wish a good time she'll find it for you. She is a most surprising combination of virtues with just enough mischief mixed in.

name: Dubois, Charles Frederick
motto: "The world's no better if we worry, Life's no longer if we hurry."
description: Fred is a jolly medic whose philosophy is "Eat, drink, and be merry, for tomorrow you may—" have to diet. His conversation, too, is—well, it would be perfect, if only relieved by a few flashes of silence; and you can be sure if you see Fred winding up the watch of his wit, that sometime it will strike. Talk to him yourself and get to know a fine fellow.

name: Einarsson, Karl E.
motto: "My mirth and good humor are coin in my purse."
description: Karl has political aspirations. Ought to be successful, as his keenness and quick discernment always enable him to evade the questions of the profs. Karl's supreme ambition in life is to attain a financial career equal to that of J. Pierpont Morgan.

name: Fawcett, Margaret
motto: "The force of her own merit makes her way."
description: The reason that there are so many unsympathetic and tactless people in the world is because Peggy has more than her share. Peggy was an institution at the University and no one felt that the day was well begun without one of Peggy's smiles. Now, however, Peggy is spreading smiles in Oregon, but we hear that the Canadian demand for American smiles is becoming imperative.

name: Fischer, Edgar
high school: Valley City High School, U. S. Military Academy
motto: "Not flashy, but glowing always."
description: A gilt-edged piece of humanity. His unpretentious manner, his kindness and good-fellowship have won for him the friendship of all his associates. Eggie once yearned to be a soldier, but after a few months at West Point his love of peace overrode the patriotism of youth.

name: Frederick, S. Leslie
high school: Plymouth High School, Upper Iowa University, Carnegie College, Lenox College
motto: "If waiting on his utterance, the fate of nations hung, Fateless they'd all grow old."
description: Slow of speech, but sure of step. The Y. M. C. A. has led him, as its President, through a tempestuous year. His favorite joke at the Commons: "Will you have soup with or without?" "Without what?" "Thumbs, sir."

name: Fannie, Reinette Friendberg
motto: "Of all the arts, great music is the art To raise the soul above all earthly storms."
description: And no one realizes this more than Fannie does. She not only appreciates music, but she also helps others to do so by her interpretations. Her nature is just as full of harmony as are her several original productions. To this we must add that a poorly prepared lesson on Fannie's part would strike a discord in her nature.

name: Annetta, E. Fried
motto: "Of soul sincere, in action faithful, and in honor clear."
description: The crack center of the winning Junior Basketball Team in 1912. Noted, too, for her skill in the Howe and Why of cooking class, and for her faithful work on the Y. W. Cabinet.
text:

name: Hildegarde, U. Fried
motto: "In framing an artist, art hath thus decreed, to make some good, but others to exceed."
clubs: Vice-President of Y. W. C. A.
description: Hildegarde is one of the latter type. In sketching, in designing, in tinting, in oil or china painting, in ceramics, Hildegarde excels. Besides, she is a student. She has gained her goodly share of A's and B's. And yet she has "lots of time," which she spends in being sociable.

name: Glaspel, Cyril J.
motto: "Could I love less, I should be happier now."
description: A sharp-tongued fellow is Cy, with a touch of cynicism in his make-up and an unquenchable thirst for love. He is cute, a nifty dresser, and plays the fussing game at two ends. He is a good student; a clever, keen-witted fellow, and an authority on sports.

name: Graber, Ava
motto: "A paradox who puzzles when she pleases and pleases when she puzzles."
description: It was once upon a time rumored that Ava caught the eight o'clock car, but on further investigation we found that the car was a half hour late. However, Ava's faults of tardiness and changing her mind are so mixed with her many virtues that they make her a most delightful bit of femininity.

name: Graham, Milton Peter
motto: "So sweet a smile was never found In this sad world of ours."
description: Yes, Girls, this is Milton; not the other one. Aren't you glad the Dacotah is out, so that you can get the Graham puzzle straightened out? It is a pity that a fellow so handsome and so affable should waive all attentions from the girls. A great lover of humanity—he nearly always brings a second order of pie. Avaunt, Miss Treen!

name: Graham, Robert William
motto: "All men have their faults; too much modesty is his."
description: It is always a pleasure to meet Bob on the campus; he is always in good humor. In spite of his modesty, he has been unable to keep hidden his sterling qualities of character. "We heard of this man, and good words came with his name."

name: Graham, John C.
motto: "There ain't no use to grumble and complain; It's just as cheap and easy to rejoice."
clubs: Vice-President and Secretary, Athletic Association, '13 and '14
description: The Falstaff of our University drama. Likewise a power behind the throne who sometimes appears in front of the curtain. John is one of the few steady souls who can withstand popularity. A possessor of sane ideas upon current questions, a man with the strength to live as he believes.
text:
"There ain't no use to grumble and complain; It's just as cheap and easy to rejoice."
The Falstaff of our University drama. Likewise a power behind the throne who sometimes appears in front of the curtain. John is one of the few steady souls who can withstand popularity. A possessor of sane ideas upon current questions, a man with the strength to live as he believes.

name: Haig, Rena
motto: "She speaks, behaves and acts just as she ought."
description: One of the leading lights of Macnie Hall. An earnest student who is not sparing of the midnight tallow either for study or spreads. If more of us had her conscientiousness mayhap professors would be more gracious and less prodigal with deficiency slips.

name: Harris, John G.
motto: "Begone, old Care, and I prithee begone from me, For i' faith, old care, thee and I shall never agree."
description: "From Saddle to College" is the title of Jack's biography. He "packed" himself here four years ago, and ever since has "packed" the joys and sorrows of every one at the U. Carefree and happy. With a heart of gold and his politician smile one finds it impossible not to like him. His specialty is Debating Board receptions.

name: Harvey, Gladys Marie
high school: Ada, Minnesota, High School; Ladies Seminary, Red Wing, Minnesota
motto: "Her heart, her talents, and her hands Are free to all who need her."
description: Ada, Minn., has won renown at U. N. D. by former representatives, but it remained for this "sharkiest" of "sharks" to make it famous. Wrapt in the solitude of her own originality, "she stands divinely fair, and most divinely tall." Her literary fame will not be bounded by the walls of Merrifield 203. She is going to revolutionize the short story.

name: Hofto, Jacob Arnold
motto: "I'm up and down and round about, Yet all the world can't find me out."
description: "Jake" is a mystery of mysteries to those who do not know him, but to those to whom he gives his friendship he is a prince of good fellows. A man who lives, speaks, and thinks in big things. Debates, sings, or "orates" equally well. Is blissfully ignorant of but one thing at the University—co-eds.

name: Houghton, Theodosia
motto: "Sae jolly and sweet, sae full complete, She steals our affections away."
description: Dosia's charm and winning manner have made her the friend of everyone on the campus for she realizes that to have friends one must be a friend. Dosia has also a great deal of ingenuity. How do we know it? Because she has lately discovered that with the proper training even a girl—and a senior at that—can make Axles out of Planks.

name: Igawa, Takeo
motto: "To thee, Japan, thy sturdy sons remain forever true."
description: "Tok" is our only representative from the land of the Rising Sun. The best-hearted fellow in the world, and as popular with faculty as with his fellow-students. His ability at exploring the depths of all problems augurs well for his future success at his profession.

name: Irwin, Florence Temple
motto: "She leads one on—but while she leads—she never looks behind."
description: Temple was one of the cleverest and strongest girls at the U. Full of lofty ambitions and the enterprise to realize them. She received her degree in three years and a half and is now doing Graduate work at Wisconsin. Broad minded and far seeing to an unusual degree, Temple is sure to lead and succeed in whatever she undertakes.

name: Johnson, John Bertrand
motto: "He looks the whole world in the face."
major: Arts, Hesperia, Chairman Budge Hall Council, Fellowship in Physics
description: Bert can lecture on the electromagnetic theory of light, differential equations, or modern tendencies in the appreciation of Shakespeare. In addition he is an Ole Bull with the violin. Does nothing unconventional, for that means living without effort. Though a Scan., he will be an American scientist of the German type.

name: Joslyn, Edna Muriel
motto: "To know her is to love her."
description: Edna Joslyn—a student. On record as having a straight A credit book. Do you know her? Then you'll agree that she's a girl in a thousand. Edna enjoys a good time as only those who have worked hard can. Her mathematical genius has not been cultivated at the expense of friends.

name: Kneeshaw, Robert Stanley
motto: "Like Atlas he bears the weight of the world upon his shoulders."
description: "Stan" has been a pillar of strength in every University activity and the Gibraltar of many. Also, because he "plays romantic parts with feeling" he has won fame as an actor. In odd moments he has made himself master of his calling. As a freshman, he was challenged by Cupid and has been able to take a dare ever since.

name: Lampert, E. Margaret
motto: "‘Tis the mind that shows in every act, And chiefly in her roguish e'en."
description: An optimist of the first order. Margaret can carry more work and collect more A's per semester and say less about it than anyone in the institution. A willing and never-tiring assistant in the library for students looking for unheard-of data. As a friend, always sympathetic or jolly as one's mood demands.

name: Locklin, Clarence D., Jr.
motto: "If he be not in love with some woman, There is no believing old signs; a' brushes his hat o' mornings; what should that bode?"
description: To the casual observer it would be hard to tell whether Dave lived in Grand Forks or Rolla. He is now engaged in the occupation of relieving the world of some of its surplus wealth, having absorbed sufficient law for this purpose.

name: Lohn, Leon
motto: "He was a scholar, and a ripe and good one; exceeding wise, fair-spoken, and persuading; and to those men that sought him sweet as summer."
description: He takes himself very seriously, and, really, it is a very serious matter indeed. Is never seen except going to or coming from Economics classes or buried behind books on the tariff or banking. But if one has the courage to stop him in his ceaseless pilgrimages or knock over the books, a fine true friend will be found beneath it all.

name: Louden, J. Earl
motto: "I see him strain on tiptoe, soar and pour eloquence out, nor stay nor stint at all."
description: Louden is not a loud 'un, on the contrary, he's a great success at minding his own business. When his business happens to be University debates, he does cut loose and becomes the "great noise." His record shows how well he works; in recreation his comradeship is satisfaction and his keenness of wit is an incentive.

name: McDermott, Annie Orinda
motto: "Her ways are ways of pleasantness."
description: Annie decided as a freshman that she had absorbed all the knowledge U. N. D. had to offer. But one semester at Minnesota proved to her that there was still much worth knowing at North Dakota. Arts '13, was glad to welcome her back as one of its most loyal members, as her dainty, gentle ways had gained her many friends.

name: McGuire, Daniel Cleary
motto: "Work! where did I hear that word before?"
description: Does he really know or is he bluffing? We can't tell,—professors also refuse to definitely answer the question. Always ready with an ingenious explanation on any matter or phenomenon. Mac knows his Math and can do amusing little tricks in Physics Lab., but the strain of keeping pace with him and his thirst for knowledge has worn out two professors.

name: McKnight, John Howard
motto: "Well I'll grant you that."
description: Bunny is noted for many things; he manipulates his social obligations cleverly, he rags wonderfully, his ability in argument is remarkable, and—well, who hasn't heard about the famous twenty-yard punt. In fact, on his own testimony, "he's there!"

name: Monley, Gorie Michael
motto: "What's his history? A blank, my Lord."
description: We believe Mike did create enough energy by his sophomore year to fuss a co-ed, but the effort was too much. Again he has subsided into oblivion. Considers writing a book on "Little Cuts to Classes."

name: Oeschger, Gretchen Helen
high school: Grand Forks High School, Smith College
motto: "Made up of wisdom and of fun Medley of all that's dark and clear Of all that's foolish, all that's dear."
description: Gretchen, at the end of her sophomore year, realizing that old ties were strongest and also the unwritten fate of the majority of Smith girls, came back to help make more illustrious the class of thirteen. A scientific shark of the first degree, she is a "real comfort," an enthusiastic tennis player fully realizing the meaning of "love all."

name: Pardee, Artland Lynn
motto: "The towering mind, self-centered stood, Nor wanted man's opinion to be great."
description: The president of Sayre—but very human when once you come to know him. If "persistency" is a "jewel," Pardee ought to be a scintillating diamond. Has strong humanitarian tendencies.

name: Parizek, William Jennings
high school: Lidgerwood High School, Wahpeton Science School
motto: "Describe him if you can."
description: Bill is a good fellow, carefree and immune from worry. Is responsible for certain flashes of real wit,—the kind that makes you smile gently. For certain reasons he chose to desert the Engineer class of '12 and joined the Arts corps of unlucky '13. Lucky for the class, but what about Bill?

name: Patmore, Maud Howard
motto: "All good luck and happiness."
description: It isn't always the best people that are the easiest to write up. Now, what would you say of Maud? A fusser? She has too much sense. A grind? Hardly. She knows too well 'tis the early bird that gets tired first. A shark? She would loathe to be the abomination of the rest of the class. What then? Just a jolly fellow—that is all.

name: Monta, M. Perry
motto: "Were silence golden, I'd be a millionaire."
description: If "Monte" knew how hard it is to "write him up," he would certainly have cracked a joke, gone fussing, or missed a Y. M. C. A. meeting to furnish some material. We know, though, that he is a conscientious student and could not be more honest. One of the "faithful" on Men's Day at League Tea.

name: Edward, F. Porter
motto: "His years but young, but his experience old; his head unmellowed, but his judgments ripe."
description: He is a writer, speaker, scholar, gentleman and friend. Also he is the original Gibson man. On top of other and equally flattering successes, he has won the Rhodes Scholarship to Oxford. When Ned cuts loose with his erudite Graeco-Latin-English, he will make even the fossils at Oxford crawl out of their holes and gasp for ozone.

name: Ernest, Kenneth Read
high school: Mankato High School, Grand Forks High School
motto: "He could demonstrate mathematically that you couldn't see what you saw."
description: An authority on motionless moving picture manipulations. Knows engineering from washing machines to automobiles. Has the endurance record for talking. Likewise, he has had more adventures and been the leading man of more tales than the hero of the most thrilling best seller.

name: Harris, Robinson
motto: "A sympathetic soul is he, Free of care and deviltry."
description: Here is a man worth knowing. Robbie has made good in baseball, basketball, and football, and is a shark in carpentry. He spends much of his time in the library, working of course. One of those "comfortable" fellows, who never grates on the nerves.

name: Sagen, George A.
motto: "My only books are women's looks."
description: And George is an A student though he professes to be a woman-hater and an anti-suffragette. However, if properly approached, he could probably be induced to vote for women's rights. Nevertheless, he hasn't a care in the world. A man who will neither train for a race nor race for a train.

name: Schlosser, Anna May
high school: Mayville High School, Mayville Normal, Illinois Women's College
motto: "The queen of a thousand for grace."
description: A girl with fine executive ability combined with the happy faculty of taking things easy. Anna May believes that "a greasy grind gathers no friends", and avoids the midnight candle. Her specialties are economics and track work. "Who is that stunning couple?" (Quoted from any freshman.)

name: Schlosser, Walter Howard
motto: "Cheerier than the cherry, Rudder than the berry."
description: From a Tony Lumpkin of High School days to President of the Athletic Board as a senior is a far cry, but Walter has accomplished this feat with all the grace of a modern Sir Raleigh. Now that he has started on a fresh career as a lawyer, we hold our breath for what is to come.

name: Schultze, Max Frederick
motto: "You beat upon your pate and fancy wit will come, Knock as you please, there's nobody at home."
description: "Dutch" came to us from the University of Minnesota. He lays claim to being a vocalist of some note, just what note has not been determined. His favorite diversion is to sit with his feet on the radiator, light his pipe, and entertain the "boys" with "short humorous" experiences.

name: Shideler, Caroline M.
motto: "So gracefully sweet-tempered, so domestic, joyful, busy, and light-hearted."
description: Caroline does not go in for "Women's mission, Women's rights, Woman's wrongs, and everything that is Woman's with a capital W." She is specializing in domestic science and says she intends to interest others in the work. Perhaps! but we hear that there is One with an unusual interest.

name: Cena, Lavina Sprague
motto: "A friendship that like love is warm, A love like friendship steady."
description: Cena has an art of making and keeping friends that we all envy. She is one of those substantial dependable girls that one likes to find when something is to be done right away. She doesn't mix much in politics, but it is generally understood that on election day she votes THE straight ticket.

As a certain person was heard to remark: "He has a perfectly ponderous brain." Can discuss with perfect equanimity anything from the Pythagorean proposition to Mendel's Law of Heredity.

name: Lars, Wambem
motto: "On their own merits, modest men are dumb."
description: A man not attracted by the glitter of gold nor dazzled by the shine of the coronet; a man with ability, yet with no desire to make of it a pretentious display. He is truly a sincere devotee of the simple life and would prefer a quiet corner in the Scan room to leading the Senior Ball.

motto: "Solid pudding against empty praise."
description: Oscar is a quiet unassuming fellow who takes the brunt of battle and the laurels of victory with the same unpretentious manner. He has been a steady player on the Varsity basketball team for three years and has more than once saved us from defeat.

high school: East Grand Forks, Model High School
motto: "Not only good—but good for something."
description: If one wants a thing well done, Mike is always to be called on. His past record can stand inspection. He has tried nearly every line of University activity, and is looking for other fields. His chief problem is: "After college—what? The stage or the pulpit?"

name: Rowe, Etta M.
high school: Hamilton High School, Model High School
motto: "Ever a true woman and true friend."
description: Etta is one of the "bright, illuminating lights" in the domestic science department. If she continues as diligent in her culinary experiments as now, we prophesy an immediate demonstration that "The way to a man's heart is through his stomach."

name: Austinson, Joseph B.
motto: "The important thing in life is to have a great aim, and to possess the aptitude and perseverance to attain it."
description: Joe is a typical senior of the old type, very dignified. He pleads his case well, and has the appearance of a lawyer who knows his business. He should make a good lawyer in this north country, as his hair is light and his name ends with son.

name: Brockhoff, Fred J.
motto: "Friends, Romans, countrymen, lend me your ears."
description: Brock is a Dutchman with a sense of humor. He always has some innocent non-combatant in the corner telling him a funny story,—at least he says it is funny. He says that when he graduates and makes his pile he is going to build an air line to Larimore Hall, with a view to extending it into the Northwood country.

name: Eastman, Dan Victor
motto: "The old adage about 'too many irons in the fire' conveys an abominable lie. Keep them all going—poker, tongs, and all."
description: Vic does keep them all going. He is a farmer, he runs a threshing outfit, and goes to law school. He thinks that threshing out other people's troubles is going to be more profitable than threshing their grain. At least, since he is going to be a lawyer, he ought to be able to collect his threshing bills.

name: Elken, Clarence L.
motto: "When I was very young, I used to say 'I.'"
description: Clarence has been a respecter and enthusiast of law from earliest childhood, when law was first administered by hand. To be consistent, Elken now reverently regards law as infallible, and the Constitution the evolved masterpiece of the ages. To feminine inquirers—Elken is never home between 7:30 P. M. and 9 A. M.

name: Foote, Stanley Ralph
motto: "He takes his learning by the foot (e) measure."
description: Although Ralph is quiet, for a lawyer, he gives one the impression that if he would but unbend he could say great things. A quiet man, and from Minot—it seems a paradox. Has his superior wisdom led him to profit by the example of the usual Minot product, or does he expect to start a reform there?

name: Albert, B. Hemp
motto: "You must run to win the race."
description: And Al has won many a race. Al is one of those fellows who doesn't say much except when there is something to say. If he is as much of a sticker as he has been in all branches of University athletics, he is sure to make good.

name: Lembke, Frank Theodore
motto: "Happy is the man that findeth wisdom."
description: We call him Dad, and he well deserves the name. He has a deep sonorous voice and a serious manner. When he makes a recitation it sounds as if he were offering a prayer that the prof. be endowed with more common sense. Sits in the married men's row, and seems to be watching us kids with calm patience.

name: Maher, Howard
motto: "To Hell with the faculty."
description: Immaculate and clean-cut in every respect, Howard always makes a good impression. His among-those-present average at social functions is well above the 400 mark. Whether presiding as toastmaster at a banquet, handling a billiard cue or pushing a drawing pencil, he is equally accomplished.

name: Donald, Bruce McDonald
motto: "He's a braw Scot laddie, an he kens the lassies weel."
description: Mack is the soldier boy of the bunch; he almost stationed a company at Grand Forks. A fair student and a maker of wonderful speeches on gala occasions. Expects to set up business in some small North Dakota town, presumably Casselton.

name: Ross, McIntosh
motto: "For he's a jolly good fellow."
description: Ross will make a success of life because he is Scotch and always has something laid away for a rainy day. (His McIntosh). He is a companionable sort of fellow, but he likes the Scotch best of all. His ability as a student and bluffer is proved by the fact that he has successfully run the terrible gauntlet of the Law faculty.

name: O'Reilly, John Donovan
motto: "With the grace of an Irishman."
description: Don is a Canuck who came down to study law. He also figures that living in the United States will give him a little more grace than he could acquire by staying in Canada. He has lots of snap and a ready wit, and should make the Canadians take notice when he goes back with his sheepskin. He will probably be able to skin a few sheep for himself.

name: Poole, William G.
motto: "I never knew so young a body with so wise a head."
description: Bill is the William Burns of the Law School. If there is anything you want to find out about the school ask him. He even knows when the profs are cooking up some deal to raise the standard of the school, and consequently lower the standing of the students. A good fellow, always on the square, and liked by all who know him.

name: Sad, John
motto: "He is so full of pleasant anecdotes, So rich, so gay, so poignant is his wit."
description: Jack is our star fun-maker. He can make a joke go, which with anyone else would fall flat. Jack has been at the University so long that he has come to be a fixture. How we are going to get along without our genial station agent is a problem hard to solve. One thing certain,—he goes with the best wishes of all.

name: Stambaugh, Lynn Upshaw
motto: "You may have heard this, but I'll tell it to you anyway."
description: Lynn above everything is a jolly good fellow. He has the best equipped repertoire of laughter-evoking stories of any man at the University. If he had the inclination, Lynn would be an irresistible shark at campus oratory. A handsome fellow, a semi-scholar, and a gentleman.

name: Stoudt, Karl Henry
motto: "Wit is the salt of conversation."
description: A senior and witty,—it seems impossible. Some of his bright ideas are good, but the profs. can seldom see through them. For example: Prof. Carp: "If a coat and a pair of pants were destroyed by different persons, could action for damages be brought in one action?" Karl: "Yes, if they belonged to the same suit." Isn't that enough to convict any senior?

name: Torgerson, William R.
motto: "I laugh; for hope hath happy place for me."
description: Bill is a live wire in all college activities, but is especially gifted in finance. Torgie prefers Piper Heidsick to Yucatan. He is studious, energetic, and ambitious. His choice of a future fluctuates between the presidency of the Scandinavian Bank and a financial reorganizer of the entire west.

name: Vinje, Arne
motto: "Books are the best things, well used; abused, among the worst."
description: Arne is our man of books. Knows more about the library than any man in school. He started out to be a teacher, but before it was too late decided it would be more profitable to advise men than to teach children. He will soon be able to tell us all about it.

name: Watne, Carl Augustus
motto: "Patience to work is a virtue very few possess."
description: Carl is a worker from the word go. Has had many hair-raising experiences while at the Law School, especially after the mid-year exams. He is a wonder at note book keeping, and when he graduates expects to make his start in life by selling his note books to the West Publishing Company, to be used as a new case book.

name: Batten, James Cordery
motto: "Pressed with the load of life, the weary mind surveys the general toil of humankind."
description: Socialist, anarchist, reformist, radicalist or any other —ist that means turning everything upside down. Cordery might be called a human dynamo. No social or economic problem is too deep for him and he always has a solution. Takes his attention off the world problems annually, however, and turns it to track and football.

name: Bliss, Gertrude
motto: "Measures, not men, have always been my mark."
description: An ardent follower of Whitman, she believes the secret of health and happiness is found in the great out-of-doors. The tennis game she plays is the wonder of all the girls. She may be found on the court from sunrise until sunset. Classes alone interfere.

name: Brecka, Frank Vincent
high school: State Gymnasium, Prague, Grafton High School
motto: "A Comedy of Errors."
description: Stop! Look! Listen! You couldn't escape anyhow, as Brecka has arrived at the final solution of the great part of our present day problems and feels it his duty to enlighten the race. Tilting at windmills didn't go out with Don Quixote. But by this system he has acquired much knowledge and dispensed still more.

name: Brown, Lizzie May
motto: "She never fails to speak a pleasant word."
description: A thorough, earnest, industrious student. Her ambition is to be a history teacher for she enjoys both—history and teaching. Besides having a long line of A's and B's to her credit, she also has a long line of friends.

name: Coe, Luella E.
motto: "Gracious and useful, all she does, Blessed and blessed where'er she goes."
description: She sees the deeper side of life, and the silver lining to every cloud. Though ever lost in thought, she is not lost to the lives of those about her. With her cheery ways and ready smile, she has won a warm place in the hearts of both faculty and students.

name: Collins, Frances S.
high school: Grand Forks High School, Stout Institute, Menominee, Wis.
motto: "Gentleness and kindness are virtues she doth possess."
description: Frances has come to us just this year, but if she likes us half as well as we like her she'll not hurry away. She's so affable, so natural and unassuming that she wins the friendship of everyone. Although she has already won laurels in the field of Domestic Sciences, Frances realizes the need of thorough preparation for a successful life work.

name: Covert, Emily Compton
high school: State Normal and Industrial School, Ellendale
motto: "Variety is the spice of life; that gives it all its flavor."
description: A modern mixture of Mark Twain, Socrates and Queen Elizabeth. Is silent except when she speaks her mind—which she does whenever she has a mind. Advises freely and as freely takes her own advice. Is so quaint, original and charming that from professor up—or down—to most verdant Freshman she has an unbroken line of willing friends.

name: Crain, Luella
motto: "For she was jes' the quiet kind whose natures never vary."
description: Luella is with us only about half the time. Somehow, however, she manages to keep up with us. For how could she reconcile herself to any other class? A diligent worker. Always ready and willing to help out, with anything that needs to be done.

name: Currie, Ina
motto: "Rich in saving common sense."
description: Ina is known for her rare good nature. A jolly good fellow who in time of peace prepares for exams and secures her share of A's. She has helped to make the '14 girls basketball team the champion for the last two years. She's Scotch but she can't help that, and we couldn't do without her.

name: Dahl, Elsie Kathryn
motto: "Bright as the sun, her eyes the gazers strike; and like the sun, they shine on all alike."
description: Elsie has an equally cordial smile for everyone. She is our fair Art Editor who will tell you that true art lies in the concealment of any fixed design—which maxim she practices well on her many admirers, though lately her glance has been settled due South. Elsie has another side of wondrous capability, for she has carried in a remarkable way the heavy duties of being Junior President.

name: Finch, Clarence Leland
motto: "'Tis better to have fussed and lost than never to have fussed at all."
description: Clarence's friends will testify that he is the best-hearted fellow in the world. He has been accused of being a specialist in campus-try, but, really, is that an unpardonable failing? A famous nimrod,—the only fellow we know who will walk fifteen miles, come back with nothing, and consider the time well spent.

name: Flynn, Ellen
motto: "She is Irish thru and thru."
description: Ella is one of our optimistic friends from the land of St. Patrick. She always has a pleasant word and smile for everyone. Ella enjoys Economics and would make a most successful business woman. She believes in taking the world as she finds it and letting the problems take care of themselves.

name: Forester, Joseph H.
motto: "Be calm in arguing; for fierceness makes error a fault, and truth discourtesy."
description: Joe is two-sided; his model is Theodore Roosevelt, whom he places above all, defends, and tries to imitate; and he is a near believer in Socialism. He waxes hot in argument, especially when proving "why Teddy was shot."

name: Fritz, Chester
high school: Lidgerwood High School, University of Washington
motto: "A rolling stone gathers no moss—but who wants moss?"
description: Chet makes an ideal hero—on the stage. He is one of those very likable fellows who win friendships through personality and magnetism. Inclined to be a will-o'-the-wisp. Has investigated almost every state and occupation in the Union—and is still investigating. Has sounded all the depth and breadth and height of wit—and is still sounding.

name: Gilby, F. Roy
motto: "He was more than over shoes in love."
description: What does that first initial stand for? Why, Fusser,—of course. Will any one deny that "Gibel" is the most consistent fusser at the U? He is much interested in engineering problems, and more especially those relating to the operation of the telephone. With it all, he is a hard worker, and an admirable friend.

name: Jessie, Grassick
motto: "'Twas kind o' kingdom come to look on such a blessed creetur; A dogwood blushing to a creek ain't modester nor sweeter."
description: Jessie is rarely seen at University functions, her interests are in the city. We predict an "and-so-the-Prince-and-Princess-got-married-and-lived-happily-ever-after" ending for her. She is a bonnie Scotch lassie with a genius for smiling and dancing,—and who would want a better combination.

name: Ethel Evangeline, Halcrow
motto: "Whose worth Stood challenger on mount of all the age For her perfections."
clubs: State Sec'y of College Y. P. B.
description: A stellar member of the genus Versatilus. Spectacular basket-ball, excellent literary work, earnest laborer in Y. P. B.'s vineyard, a pleasing talent for reading, and clever poster sketching—these are the five points of this star's genius. And because "Ethel" is pleasant and accommodating we rejoice to have her at our U.

name: Mabel A., Hanson
high school: Driscoll; Tobin College, Iowa; St. Olaf College
motto: "So unaffected, so composed a mind."
description: Mabel has been with us but a short time, but she has already proved her capability. A member of John Adams' Star Journalism Class of 1911-12. She has but one grievance—the Macnie girls just won't quit moving around that photo—of her brother at Harvard.

name: Herbert, Austin Harvey
motto: "Our thoughts and our conduct are our own."
description: To be an electrical engineer of world renown is Herb’s ambition. Of course, he may raise his aim when he almost reaches this one. But what an electrical engineer wants of differentials and integrals is more than he can see.

name: Hassell, Selma
motto: "A friend in need is a friend indeed."
description: No one’s troubles are ever too little or too big but what she can in some way straighten them out. She has taken everything from Law to Harmony and though she cannot choose between them, we know that she is a law unto herself and brings harmony wherever she goes.

name: Hilleboe, Grace Glenn
motto: "Laugh and the world laughs with you."
description: Grace is as merry and light-hearted a comrade as Pierrot himself could wish. She follows this motto with the natural results—an optimistic nature and a large circle of friends. She is serious too, and with the ardor and strength that she possesses will reach her goal—that of a successful artist.

name: Jensen, Ada Viola
motto: "She speaks four languages word for word, and uses nary a book."
description: Indeed, Ada is a real shark when it comes to languages. And she has not forgotten to lay proper emphasis upon English. Likewise, Ada enjoys music. Many are the times we have enjoyed her renditions of Mendelssohn and Mozart. She speaks her mind when necessary. At other times she is ready to listen while you express yours.

name: Johnson, Albert
high school: De Lamere, Park Region Luther College
motto: "There ain't no use in all this strife And hurrying pell mell through life."
description: Quiet but ambitious, young but earnest, communicating with Nature, himself and his oboe, onward through life he goes. He understands the principle of bluffing, the latest rag, and three Greek roots. Reticent he is and reticent he always will be—except concerning himself.

name: Kellogg, Frances Edith
motto: "The mildest manner, the gentlest heart."
description: A quiet maiden who comes and goes giving smiles and receiving them from every one. She pays little attention to the masculine species, though they admire her—from a distance. "Because", as the wise man, who stammers, says, "it's v-v-very m-m-much Sh-Sh-Shafer."

name: Clemens, F. Kelsch
motto: "He was concentrated knowledge—he was quintessence of college."
description: A man who has done much with his University opportunities. Kelsch had ten talents and put them all out at interest—and there is no usury law in the kingdom of knowledge. His success in public debate augurs well for his success in his profession. To keep in touch with him in after life, read the headlines. Also popular with the co-eds.

name: Vera, Kelsey
motto: "Dainty, discreet, diligent, and sweet Not much for height, but an all around delight."
description: A little girl who made a big smile famous. An idealist and altruist of the highest type. Also a playwright. From considering such dark problems as "Lighting the Gym for the Prom," to "How to Attend Six Committee Meetings at Once," Vera shows that she makes it a practice to put all her worries in the bottom of her heart—then smile.

name: Alice, Anastasia Kolars
motto: "Her very frowns are sweeter far than smiles of other maidens are."
description: Alice has a winsome dimple that comes and goes bewitchingly; and eyes—when once we've looked in them—possess us. Has A's to let, tutors her Prof. in Math. (?) and does dramatic work for a pastime. In all things and on all occasions she has an Art which never fails to captivate. But oh, Shaw, what's the use?
text:
"Her very frowns are sweeter far than smiles of other maidens are."
Alice has a winsome dimple that comes and goes bewitchingly; and eyes—when once we've looked in them—possess us. Has A's to let, tutors her Prof. in Math. (?) and does dramatic work for a pastime. In all things and on all occasions she has an Art which never fails to captivate. But oh, Shaw, what's the use?

name: Randall, J. Larson
high school: Minot High School, Luther College, Decorah, Iowa
motto: "Any little girl that's a nice little girl Is the right little girl for me."
description: One of Randall's most prominent characteristics is his aptness at cultivating any little girl, — and they all swear by him too. Randall's accomplishments are not limited, however (though you notice we do not mention his hypnotizing line of talk), for his "thereness" is something the fellows all appreciate. His philosophy is: "Knock and the world knocks with you, Boost and you boost alone."

name: Lee, Julia J.
motto: "Teach me half the gladness that thy heart must know."
description: Truly Julia is a cheery combination of good will, kindness, and jolly disposition. Her optimism and laugh are infectious. Many times her witty and original remarks have driven away dull care and trouble from our burdened souls. Her ambition is to be either a doctor or a settlement worker.

name: McDougall, Catherine Cameron
motto: "Ilka lassie has her laddie None they say I have Yet all the laddies smile at me A comin' thru the rye."
description: No need to say that Catherine is Scotch. A bonnie lass noted for her independence and decided opinions which she has the common sense to defend. She's there every time in Basketball, Committee and classroom and always ready for her share of the fun.

name: Macmaster, Hazel Edith
motto: "The girl with the smile is the girl worth while."
description: Hazel has the same gentle smile for everyone. A demure reserved maiden who took a year off to see Europe but decided she could learn more at U. N. D. and so returned to join Arts '14. Her quiet courage compels admiration. Even she herself is unconscious of the havoc wrought by her smiles in many a manly heart.

name: Markell, Minnie E.
motto: "Mary, Mary, quite contrary. How does your garden grow?"
description: If one wants Minnie to do anything the surest way to have her do it is to convince her that her only salvation lies in doing exactly opposite. However, when she does do anything, it is done in a manner which leaves no room for criticism. Minnie also has some excellent ideas all her own of things worth while.

name: Norman, Gertrude Tolene
motto: "When love and duty clash, let duty go to smash."
description: Gertrude has a passion for music and everything connected with it. Has spells of pessimism that would make indigo look white in comparison. At other times is full of mischief and fun. Though we cannot imagine it, she is planning to become a stern and dignified teacher.

name: Nyquist, Harry
motto: "Steinmetz."
description: When Harry drifted in a little late and proceeded on his two weeks' climb from Freshman to Junior, we all thought he was a combination tank and grind. However, his ready wit and certain "come backs" soon made him a favorite. He is not so pious as he looks and can say "Goodness Gracious!" with most startling vehemence.

name: Owen, Owen Tudor
motto: "A politician, fitly planned To scheme, to graft, but to command."
description: An outspoken fellow with enough radicalism to question the rights of convention. Is in politics for the love of victory. The power behind the throne in most elections. When not in politics or directing the University's policy, does duty as a press agent.

name: Plank, Laurance Robbins
motto: "A noble life before a long."
description: We consider Laurance to be pretty good timber. He amuses himself by pretending that he is of a lazy, leisurely, dilettante disposition. In fact, he is not only a student, but a scholar. He is a good writer, an artistic actor, and in the band he excels with "the ready sweetness" of his clarinet.

name: Poupore, Anne E.
motto: "She is a very superior young lady of very remarkable attractions, graces and virtues."
description: As a certain professor remarked, "Anything Miss Poupore does is bound to be excellent." Anne writes debates, wins A's, dances, and does it all equally well. When you want a thing accomplished, Anne will do it. It is the busiest people who can find time to be a bit busier.

name: Powers, Lester T.
motto: "Youth comes but once in a lifetime."
description: "Elty" hopes some day to be an engineer—civil or otherwise. Is at present improving a claim in Riverside Park. In his odd moments he manages to develop an abnormal bump of mathematical knowledge.

name: Purdon, Florence
high school: Wahpeton High School, State Science School
motto: "Would that I could see more closely those hidden thought beauties of thy mind so closely veiled by thine own sweet reserve."
description: Florence came to us as a Junior. She is skillful in all out-door sports from tennis to horse-back riding. She also has the faculty of getting lessons as easily and well as she plays. She is by no means as quiet as her reserved manner indicates and is filled with unexpected ideas, deep and romantic.

name: Henry, Qualheim
motto: "Moderate of speech, with ever pleasing mien, the world he greeted with a smiling face."
description: Moderate in all things. Henry always maintains an even composure of temperament. His pupils will say of him, "Our Prof's just great. He's never grouchy, and always willing to help us with our work." His many friends testify to his get-along-with-able-ness.

name: Rasmussen, Edna Mae
motto: "Brevity of expression is proof of wisdom."
description: Edna expresses herself in an inverse ratio to her height and she is almost six feet. She has been compared to Solomon because of her "wit, words, wisdom and worth." The sternest of professors have been known to falter before her deep, searching questions. Like Solomon, Edna has a court of her own in which she hears and decides all the cases of the Carrington delegation.

name: Read, George S.
motto: "Diverse men have diverse recreations and exercises."
description: Although he has defended the class of '13 in numerous contests for inter-class honors, George found rival attractions in their successors. He is taciturn and shows a preference for travel, for practical life, and for—the opposite sex. A problem in electricity or one of the fair ones is always in his case.

name: Barnes, Lyle Rich
motto: "His words are bonds, his oaths are oracles, his love sincere, his thoughts immaculate."
description: Lyle is a prolonged silence, six feet of quiet. His modesty makes him a delightful companion and a satisfying friend. We look for him to make a "big noise" in medicine, in spite of his unobtrusiveness; it seems that there must be a place in the world looking for a modest doctor.

name: Rorke, Eva A.
motto: "Two Irish eyes of a tender hue."
description: Eva has never been known to keep quiet for five consecutive minutes. Furthermore, we wouldn't ask it of her. Such a flow of happiness and genuine Irish wit could not and ought not to be stopped. Eva not only talks, she also sings; she made an efficient choregus for the class of 1914.

name: Roy, Theodore
motto: "Strong in will, and rich in wisdom."
description: "Ted" possesses the rare gift of being dependable. He has burned many a candle at the shrine of knowledge but not in vain. He is a favorite with all who know him—though he allows few that privilege. He bears the distinction of being the only surviving member of the class to enter in 1910.

name: Elif, Rue
motto: "Smooth runs the water where the brook runs deep."
description: Elif came to us from St. Olaf. Their loss was U. N. D.'s gain. He is a good student and an enthusiast in athletics. He lends himself to saying little, but he works and plays with untiring energy. A happy, congenial, good-looking fellow,—though a journalist.

name: Sanders, Mary E.
motto: "A creature not too bright or good, For human nature's daily food."
description: Mary came to us direct from fair Iowa's capital city. She is sensible in all things but one—and that is in her disapproval of our Alma Mater and everything connected with it. However, we attribute this to mere change of atmosphere, and predict that as soon as she becomes acclimated, she will be one of our foremost enthusiasts.

name: Serumgard, Olga
motto: "For I'm to be Queen of the May, Mother, I'm to be Queen of the May."
description: There are several things which we must forgive in Olga. In the first place she is considered the most charming girl at the U, and in the second place she has a genius for friendships which none can gainsay. She has been accused of being the most democratic girl in school, but we suspect her of voting a straight —? ticket.

name: Smith, Clara Lois
motto: "A little maid, too bright and fair, Too strangely lovely for surprise."
description: A believer in getting out of life all it has to give. Cares? Oh yes, but what of them? Why be gloomy just because the House says, "No Grand at night." There are other things to do than pine away—e. g., go in the afternoon. Yes, we'll all agree that "Clara L." is a jolly Junior, and one who deserves a degree as a Gloom Dispeller.

name: Smith, Andrew Gabriel
motto: "For he's a jolly good fellow."
description: And like the angel Gabriel, he blows his own horn, long and enthusiastically. But for all his trumpeting everyone knows that "Windy" is a true friend with the biggest heart in the world. A spasmodic fusser, for (alas, Windy) thy other name is inconstancy.

name: South, Harry Ellsworth
motto: "Now he was ruddy—and goodly to look to."
description: Surely David had nothing on Sunny South when it comes to cheerfulness. If you ever feel indigo blue, just find Harry and ask him to sing you some of his funny songs; they are more potent than pills. Of his ability as a writer and organizer this book speaks volumes, and, we suspect, his future also is spoken for.

name: Spillane, Harriet
motto: "Here's to you who can always see the silver lining to every cloud."
description: Harriet, in spite of her red hair, has a gentle, modest disposition. Yet if you know her you will soon discover that the temper usually accompanying the crimson crown is not buried so deep after all. Her witty remarks prove her a daughter of old Erin.

name: Steel, F. Douglas
motto: "Quiet in class, but powerful loud in grade."
description: Did you ever see him in a hurry, or out of breath, or excited about anything? Did he ever shout or laugh boisterously? A man who hasn't slipped a heart beat in the last three years! As steady as an eight day clock, and about as liable to do anything unusual.

name: Steinbar, Minnie
motto: "A winning way, a pleasant smile."
description: Minnie's student life extends over two sides, this side and the east side. It is her ambition to become a scholar, but her profs. are satisfied if she attends classes. One week she was not marked absent a single day---but that was during vacation. In spite of her studious tendencies, Minnie is a "good fellow" as everybody knows.

name: Taylor, Fred Ellery
motto: "Every inch a man."
description: And that applies to every one of his 72 inches. He fusses only in a nonchalant manner, while at the U, and therefore remains a contented and industrious student. We have suspicions about his vacations, judging from the candy he receives from parcel post. He's a true democrat. In other causes, save your breath and guard your head when talking to Fritz.

name: Templeton, Francis H.
motto: "To be merry best becomes you; for out of question, you were born in a merry hour."
description: This is the boy with the cheerful grin and the naughty eyes. He is a shark in mathematics, and were it not for his incorrigible laziness, the brain reels to think what he might do. But just suppose he should ever start to work—what would happen?

name: Thordarson, Solveig S.
high school: Edinburg, Model High School, Valley City Normal
motto: "Have more than thou showest, Speak less than thou knowest."
description: One of Iceland's sturdy daughters, ambitious, a hard worker, and a possessor of a great deal of common sense. However, she knows how to take a joke, even though she seldom springs one. Solveig has chosen the "medical profession" as her life work and we believe she has the stick-to-it-iveness to win out.

name: Tompkins, Charles Ray
motto: "A gentleman, and a scholar."
description: He has won honors, not in athletics nor in public address, but in the classroom. He is first of all a student, then a good fellow and a desirable friend. Too bad—for them—that the girls don't know this handsome man better.

name: Traynor, Mack V.
high school: Grand Forks: Lanark, Ontario, High School
motto: "What he calls luck is simply pluck."
description: One of those fellows who "look the whole world in the face" but keeps his own shut. If there is anything to be done, Mack will do it better than anyone else; when there is nothing to be done,—same result. As advertising manager he has been a compound of bulldog, hypnotist, and just plain business.

name: Twing, Sidney Hubert
motto: "One who can think and say and do."
description: A man of few words but words of great meaning. After being three years at the University one would at least suppose him to be aware that this is a co-educational institution. However, he gives no sign. He makes up for this by brilliancy in other lines, especially Chemistry.

name: Walker, Gertrude
motto: "Gentle of speech, beneficent of mind."
description: Gertrude joined our class last year. The reason, of course, is simple. She heard of the fame of the class of '14 and thought it wise to join our ranks. We are sure Gertrude will make an able teacher, for her gentle manner, her patience, her pleasant voice, and her earnest work bespeak for her success.

name: Whitford, William Charles
motto: "Loaded with conscientious thoroughness."
description: William is one of the few men at the U who are preparing to teach. We bespeak success for him, for he is earnest in his preparation and knows what he is about. Did he not teach last year to gain his bearings? He is thorough, industrious, but not pedantic, conscientious, and altogether capable.

name: Wright, Avice Rae
motto: "That's a woman who observes and reflects in an uncommon manner."
description: Avice delights in Mathematics. So would all of us if those knotty problems would untangle so easily for us. Avice has a natural ability for settling disputes. In her own way, she decides the question to suit her own views, and yet leaves everybody happy.

name: Boostrom, Edward C.
motto: "The force of his own merit makes his way."
description: Boostrom is a worker who takes the profs very seriously. In fact, he seems to have too much faith in them, for when he tells them their own law in an exam and they won’t take it he turns from being a "Booster" to a knocker.

name: Callahan, Bernard B.
motto: "The Irish we have with us always."
description: Callie is the original "show-me-kid." Insists that the profs. earn their salary. They have to have good solid reasons for their statements before they hand him anything. Bernie has the regular Irish brogue when he desires to use it. We advise him to leave North Dakota when choosing his juries.

name: Dawson, Claude Leon
motto: "There are few, very few, who will admit defeat in argument."
description: Dawson is the receiver of all arguments, he will take either side of any question and argue it to a finish. If ousted into a corner by his opponent’s logic he can immediately start all over again; he simply refuses to be beaten. A corporation lawyer in the making.

name: Espeland, Geo. O.
high school: Milnor; Valley City Normal, Chillicothe Business College, Chillicothe, Mo.
motto: "Silence at the proper season is wisdom, and better than speech."
description: George is a quiet man; he never talks unless he has something to say. The fact that he studies hard doesn't seem to keep him from using his head. Having come from Missouri, his motto is "show me," but he often reverses it and shows the other fellow.

name: Feetham, Lawrence R.
high school: Model High School, Grand Forks High School.
motto: "Be bold, but not too bold; be strong, but not head-strong."
description: Larry is a comfortable sort of fellow to have around. Although he has opinions of his own, he doesn't air them except when necessary. As a usual thing, the quickest way to find him is to look in front of Larimore for a motorcycle.

name: Frebel, Fred B.
motto: "The mind ought sometimes to be diverted, that it may return the better to thinking."
description: In Fred we have a master at independent thinking; books are the least of his troubles. He has reason sufficient unto himself for every statement. Moreover, he can usually make the profs see the logic of his argument. Incidentally, Freddie's line of argument seems to carry great weight with the co-eds.

name: Halpern, Samuel Paul
motto: "At pleading cases day by day would try, and even judge and jury would defy."
description: Sam is wide-awake. He will make an up-to-the-minute lawyer. Some people accuse him of being a wind-jammer, but this need not affect his choice of a profession. That he is a business man is evidenced by the fact that he obtained a corner in Agency books this year.

name: Hammond, John C.
motto: "He that loseth wealth loseth much, he that loseth friends loseth more, but he that loseth spirit loseth all."
description: There is not much chance that Jack will lose much, for he has a wealth of friends, and his spirit is superlative. He is a man that won't sit still and let anyone slip one over on him without letting them hear about it, and in a way they won't forget.

name: Jacobson, Ray A.
motto: "All solitary enjoyments quickly pall."
description: Jakie will never be in any danger of having his enjoyments pall on him, for the major part of his time is spent with his friends. A very steady fusser; he does much to make the University attractive to the co-eds. Ray has developed a wonderful accuracy in the interpretation of puzzling law writings.

name: Rheinhart, J. Kamplin
motto: "As the Mississippi is deep, strong and steady, truly he was a great man."
description: Kamplin is a Grand Forks product and he does her proud. He is a deep student and an orator of no mean ability. As a judge he would make a hit; dignified and learned. Just think of Kamplin in a wig and gown.

name: Lebacken, E. Clifton
motto: "I am no proud Jack, like Falstaff, but a Corinthian, a lad of mettle, a good boy."
description: A man of quiet nature and a student of some renown. As a result of early disappointments in matrimonial affairs, "Mat" has decided to take a claim and settle down far from this world of suffragettes.

name: McIlraith, Edward John
motto: "The chiefest action for a man of spirit is never to be out of action."
description: Ed seems to be a regular dynamo; he is always busy. In spite of the fact that he is sometimes in the minority in his vastly original ideas on different phases of the law, he is never at a loss for a reason to back up his argument. Mac aspires to be manager of a big league team, and is taking law in order that he may make a profitable contract.

name: Mason, Milton Harold
motto: "Speech is great, but silence is greater."
description: Mitt is surely the great one of our class, both in his size and in his silence. It takes a worthy cause to move him to speech. For such trivial things such as questions which arise in class he has a silent contempt. We predict that he will do great things when he gets started.

name: Metzger, Ivan
motto: "He who is firm and resolute in will moulds the world to himself."
description: Metz says he has never sought any honors save the respect of the people. There is no doubt about his having it, for even though his ideas may sometimes conflict with ours, we have to admit that Metz is a worthy and sincere opponent. A champion of the right of free speech, and of the inalienable right of the people to govern themselves.

name: Olson, Oscar O.
high school: Central High School, St. Paul; Globe Business College, St. Paul
motto: "He that keeps an account with knowledge banks at the best bank."
description: O. O. is a banker, and his balance is good, both in his actual work and in his school work. He is of a quiet disposition, but he is not afraid to make himself heard in a good cause. If he ever starts out to be a banker and a lawyer, he will surely own a town before he dies.

name: Palmer, Archie
motto: "Our Jerry's a salad, for in him we see Oil, vinegar, and saltness all agree."
description: A product of western North Dakota who says he has seen a great deal of life. His ability to deliver a dissertation on any subject whatsoever would lend to support his claim. A man of very original ideas and set opinions, which some insist on calling stubbornness.

name: Preble, Oscar
motto: "It is by vivacity and wit that man shines in company."
description: Peb is a product of our western plains. When he first hit the University he was a perfect Remington type of cowpuncher. He is still quick on the draw, but his weapon has changed from his old six-shooter to unfailing wit and ever ready smile.

name: Russell, Roy William
motto: "A good reputation cannot be lived down."
description: All Roy's friends say he is a prince of a good fellow. The Law Editor forgot to write him up, hence we must do it in a hurry. If you want to know more,— ask Jerry G.

name: La Rue, L. Shaw
high school: Mandan High School, Blair Academy, Blairstown, New Jersey
motto: "Good nature is stronger than tomahawks."
description: In spite of his Frenchy sounding name, Larry is a typical Irishman. He is always cheery, and with a ready answer. Moreover, he is never afraid of work. While he never tried for the Glee Club, LaRue has won considerable fame as a singer,—from his prowess at the Carney Song Contest. Incidentally, you might call him a fusser.

name: Lester, M. Smith
motto: "Speech is the vehicle of thought."
description: But one glance at Lester's record will convince any doubter that he can say things in the right way. When in the eighth grade he made his start in oratory in the "One Hoss Shay," but, judging from the start he has made, his fame will endure many years longer than were allotted to that vehicle.

name: John, A. Stormon
motto: "Oh, you dear delightful women."
description: John seems to make a great hit with girls, but can you wonder at that after seeing how handsome he is? He has a most engaging blush, and has lots of chance to use it in the Law School. A scholar, a politician, and a fusser, what town will he not make a hit in?

name: Swenson, Hjalmer Wm.
motto: "Small beginnings at talking often end by producing a great orator."
description: Swenson is one of our foremost advocates of the literary life. The results, as anyone who has heard him express himself will testify, have been noteworthy. A student, a politician, and an athlete of no mean ability. Swen is a true son of his forefathers, and will no doubt make a large mark in the affairs of the state.

name: Wold, Edgar Oliver
motto: "Since silence is golden, he must be a rich man."
description: Wold is a new man at this school. Very quiet and a good student. From what the law school knows of the name and fame of the Wold tribe, you would never think that he sailed under that name; he's altogether too modest.
